text: 2026-07-24 triage: the red nights since 07-17 are a NEW failure set (old callr/arrayfire-skip causes stayed fixed). 14 failures reproduced and fixed locally: (1) vendored backends/ source trees defeat skip-guards on the GPU-less runner -> added amatrix_backend_status available-guards in test-conformance.R (MLX QR cache keys), test-opencl-model-core.R (x3), test-regression-arrayfire-worker-crash.R (x2; an outcome-conditioned skip was corrected to an all-unavailable condition so a functional-but-erroring runtime still fails); (2) AMATRIX_COVERAGE_STRICT was job-wide, leaking the intentionally-failing Track-3 strict coverage test into the gating step -> scoped to the continue-on-error step; (3) real bug: amatrix_benchmark_report expected the legacy baseline.csv schema (size/backend/speedup_vs_cpu) -> now maps canonical size_label/requested_backend and derives speedup from the cpu reference. Expected remaining red: exactly 1 failure (MLX benchmark worker probe inconsistency, tracked in bd-01KY9WX1MPQJZJNYHBR7SQTKBB). The snapshot step is now if-always so the next run captures its error_message. Close criterion unchanged: 7 consecutive green nights after the worker fix lands.
